name: Highbush blueberry (food medium)
description: Food-derived medium: metabolizable components of Highbush blueberry (Vaccinium corymbosum) [Fruits] from FooDB, plus a standard M9 mineral base. Organic components use presence-based uptake bounds; FooDB content retained. Measured compounds with no BiGG exchange are listed under uncovered.

provenance.citation: FooDB v2020-04-07 (Wishart DS et al., foodb.ca). Content sources: DUKE, PHENOL EXPLORER, S.Hakkinen, M. Heinonen, S. Karenlampi, H. Mykkanen, J. Ruuskanen, R. Torronen. Screening of selected flavonoids and phenolic acids in 19 berries. Food Research International 32 (1999) 345-353. https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0963996999000952, Sellappan S, Akoh CC, Krewer G. Phenolic Compounds and Antioxidant Capacity of Georgia-Grown Blueberries and Blackberries. 2002. J. Agric. Food Chem. 2002, 50, 2432-2438, Timo Hirvi and Erkki Honkanen. The arome of blueberries. J. Sci. Food Agric. 1983, 34, 992-998. DOI: 10.1002/jsfa.2740340916.
provenance.notes: Organic components mapped from FooDB compounds to BiGG via InChIKey/name. Mineral base added. Bounds presence-based; foodb_content retained. Unmapped measured compounds kept in uncovered (v2 completeness fix). IMPORTANT: composition values are taken from the archived FooDB 2020-04-07 CSV data release; FooDB's live food page no longer displays this bulk composition, so it will appear empty. Verify individual compounds via the per-component cross-reference links. Underlying content was aggregated by FooDB from USDA / DTU / Phenol Explorer.
